title: Make rootless 169.254.1.2 mapping more direct in the docs
body:
### Feature request description

At the moment, the docs point to a blog post instead of referencing this information directly
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/rootless_tutorial.md references https://blog.podman.io/2024/10/podman-5-3-changes-for-improved-networking-experience-with-pasta/

As this is from a year and a half ago, can this be considered stable now and therefore worth mentioning directly in the docs to improve visibility especial with regards to search engines.

### Suggest potential solution

Add "By default, the address 169.254.1.2 will be mapped to the host" to both the tutorial above and somewhere in the main docs.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
That is a pasta specific thing and does not apply to all network modes so it can only be mentioned under pasta specific things.
Our regular docs do document that address https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#network-mode-net
> To make the host.containers.internal /etc/hosts entry work and allow connections to the host, --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2 is passed.

As for the rootless tutorial I don't have a strong opinion, it is rather specific implementation detail if you think it is worth highlighting there sure PRs welcome.

@Luap99 Thanks for the info. I must admit I missed this part in the docs.
I wonder if a slight rephrasing to add `automatically passed` or `passed by default` for that section would be clearer. Happy to create a PR for these.
Yes PRs are welcome if you can improve the wording. As maintainer it is easy to assume things so user feedback is welcome to improve the wordings in order to make things clearer.
